messages.content: Build a Config service in Effect v3 using Context.GenericTag with typed access inside Effect.gen.
Convert this v2 Effect code to v3 idioms: `const Logger = Context.Tag<Logger>(); const program = Effect.flatMap(Logger, (l) => l.info("hi"));`
Fix this v2-era code to v3: `const Db = Context.Tag<Db>(); Effect.service(Db)`
Add a `currentUser` method to this Auth service: `const Auth = Context.GenericTag<Auth>("app/Auth"); interface Auth { readonly login: (u: string) => Effect.Effect<string> }`
What's the v3 way to declare a service that depends on another service? Show a minimal example.
Show me the v3 equivalent of `Context.Tag<HttpClient>()`. Brief example.
Implement a caching service in Effect v3 with strict types — no `any`, all events and context typed.
Refactor this v3 program to pull the env resolution into a Config service: `const program = Effect.sync(() => process.env.API_URL ?? "dev")`
Explain briefly how Context.GenericTag services work in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an Effect v3 program that records a request latency. Constraints: use Context.GenericTag for the Metrics service, strict types, no `any`.
Build a FileSystem service in Effect v3 using Context.GenericTag with typed read and write methods.
What's the v3 way to compose two services where one depends on the other? Show a minimal example.
Show me the v3 equivalent of accessing a service — the v2 code was `Effect.flatMap(Effect.service(Logger), (l) => l.info("x"))`. Brief example.
Convert this v2 Effect code to v3: `interface Rng { roll: Effect.Effect<never, never, number> } const Rng = Context.Tag<Rng>(); const prog = Effect.flatMap(Effect.service(Rng), (r) => r.roll)`
Add a health-check method to this v3 service: `interface Db { readonly query: (sql: string) => Effect.Effect<unknown[]> } const Db = Context.GenericTag<Db>("app/Db")`
Build a tiny feature-flag service in Effect v3 using Context.GenericTag with typed events and context.
Fix this v3 fragment — the tag is missing its identifier: `const Mailer = Context.GenericTag<Mailer>()`
Implement a rate-limiter service in Effect v3 with strict types — no `any`, all methods typed via the service interface.
Refactor this v3 program to move the `fetch` call into an HttpClient service: `const prog = Effect.tryPromise({ try: () => fetch("/api").then((r) => r.text()), catch: (u) => new Error(String(u)) })`
Explain briefly how multiple services compose into a Layer graph in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation.
Build a typed error channel in Effect v3 using Data.TaggedError and Effect.catchTag for a fetch-and-parse pipeline.
Convert this v2 tagged-error code to v3 idioms: `const DbErr = new Error("db"); return Effect.fail(DbErr)`
Fix this v2-era error handling to v3: `Effect.fail(new Error("not found")).pipe(Effect.catchAll((e) => e.message === "not found" ? Effect.succeed(null) : Effect.fail(e)))`
Add a retry-on-network-error branch to this v3 effect: `const fetchUser = (id: string): Effect.Effect<User, NetworkError | NotFoundError> => ...`
What's the v3 way to narrow a union error with Effect.catchTag? Show a minimal example.
Show me the v3 equivalent of `Effect.fail(new Error("boom"))`. Brief example.
Build a validator for a signup form in Effect v3 using Data.TaggedError with typed errors and strict types — no `any`.
Refactor this v3 code to use Data.TaggedError instead of raw Error classes: `class FooErr { readonly _tag = "FooErr" as const; constructor(readonly msg: string) {} }`
Explain briefly how Effect.catchTag narrows the error channel in Effect v3, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an Effect v3 program that reads a config file with typed errors. Constraints: tagged errors via Data.TaggedError, handled via Effect.catchTag.
Build a tagged-error hierarchy for a payment pipeline in Effect v3 with catchTag narrowing.
Fix this v3 fragment to use a tagged error instead of a raw Error: `return Effect.fail(new Error("timeout"))`
Add a fallback branch for a DbError in this v3 effect: `declare const listUsers: Effect.Effect<User[], DbError>`
Convert this v2 Effect code to v3 tagged-error idioms: `Effect.fail(new Error("parse")).pipe(Effect.catchAll(() => Effect.succeed(0)))`
What's the v3 way to combine Effect.catchTag with tagged errors across two effects? Show a minimal example.
Show me the v3 equivalent of rolling your own `readonly _tag = "Foo"` error class. Brief example.
Implement a tagged-error pipeline for a file-upload flow with strict types — no `any`, all events and errors typed via Data.TaggedError.
Refactor this v3 code to swap the generic Error for a tagged error: `const op = (x: number): Effect.Effect<number, Error> => x > 0 ? Effect.succeed(x) : Effect.fail(new Error("neg"))`
Explain briefly how Data.TaggedError differs from a hand-rolled tagged class, then show a working typed implementation.
Write an Effect v3 effect that decodes a JWT with typed errors. Constraints: tagged errors via Data.TaggedError, fallback via Effect.catchTag.
